title: lupinus argenteus var. rubricaulis
intro: Lupinus argenteus var. rubricaulis, also known as the Ruby Bush Lupine, is a perennial flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its vibrant red stems and spikes of purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which have been found to have various medicinal uses. The health benefits of Lupinus argenteus var. rubricaulis include its potential to aid in respiratory issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to treat colds, coughs, and sore throats.
